xml.stage: (Arthur is sitting in his treehouse reading and Francine is walking towards it)

line.speaker: Arthur Read
line.dialogue: Francine's my friend, but she can be hard to get along with. (points at Francine with his thumb, she waves)

xml.stage: On the basketball court

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (shoots a basketball) Anyone can be a sore loser, but Francine can be a sore winner.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (annoyed expression, picks up the ball and throws it down on the court) Oh, that makes me so mad!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: But you just won.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I should have won by more.

xml.stage: In front of the school

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (V.O.) And she sure likes to argue.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Is not.

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Is too!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Is not!

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Is too!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Brain, give up.

line.speaker: Buster Baxter
line.dialogue: You can't win.

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Yes, I can.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: No, you can't.

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Yes, I can!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: No, you can't!

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Can, can, can!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Can't, can't, can't!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: And this is what she's like in a good mood....

xml.stage: Intertitle
xml.speaker: DRAW!

line.speaker: Written by
line.dialogue: Joe Fallon --- Storyboard by: Eric Bergeron & Gerry Capelle

line.speaker: Binky Barnes
line.dialogue: (voice-over) Draw!

xml.stage: Francine hits the circle with Arthur's face in it with a mallet

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (yelping) Agh!

xml.stage: We now return to the story
in class, Mr. Ratburn is writing on the blackboard and a high-pitched noise made by the speaker makes several students cover their ears, Mr. Ratburn drops his chalk

line.speaker: Students
line.dialogue: Agh!

xml.stage: Cecilia Tingley fixes the microphone

line.speaker: Francis Haney
line.dialogue: Ah! Oh, thank you. Attention, students. (clears throat) Exciting...news.

xml.stage: back in class, the students appear interested

line.speaker: Mr. Haney (over the speaker)
line.dialogue: And this is the week of the teachers' charity carnival...fun, fun, fun! See your teachers perform (in his office) and don't miss my fabulous magic and sleight of hand! (the microphone squeaks, Francine covers her ears with books) Oops, that is all.

xml.stage: in the lunch room, the students are eating off trays

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: The teachers' carnival should be fun.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: As long as Ratburn doesn't do another puppet show. (arranges a sandwich so that olives look like eyes and the space the filling is in looks like a mouth, moves the sandwich to make it "talk" and speaks in a silly voice) If you don't laugh, I'll give you extra homework.

xml.stage: Arthur and Buster laugh

line.speaker: Fern Walters
line.dialogue: I disagree. (Arthur and Buster stop laughing and look awkward) I like Mr. Ratburn's shows.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (voicing the sandwich) Anyone who's smarter than a rock knows those puppet shows are boring kids' stuff. (Fern looks a bit unhappy and leaves, Francine puts the sandwich down and eats Fern's cookie.) Guess she didn't want her dessert. (Arthur and Buster look at each other and appear concerned)

xml.stage: in the hallway, Fern is at her locker and Arthur and Francine are carrying books

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Are you going to apologize to Fern?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Apologize for what?

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: You hurt her feelings.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: She'll get over it.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Aren't you worried she might get mad?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Fern? That mouse? What's she gonna do? Be quiet at me?! (Fern overhears and starts crying, Francine snarks) Ooh, so scared! (mean chuckle)

xml.stage: in front of the school, Francine walks over dribbling a basketball towards Arthur, Binky, Muffy, the Brain, Buster and Fern, who are laughing

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Hey, get out of my way, Buster! (they continue laughing and don't reply, Francine looks confused and annoyed) What are you laughing at? (they all look shocked except for Arthur who is holding a piece of paper and laughing, but then he looks up and hurriedly hides it behind his back) What's that?

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: (takes the paper) It's my cartoon.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (takes the cartoon, titled Furious Frank the Cow, and we see it animated as an arrogant cow kicking away animals as she reads) Out of my way! I'm better than everyone! I'm even better than people who are better than me! (Arthur laughs but looks awkward when she growls in anger) Is this cow supposed to be me?

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: How could it be? Frank is a boy.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Admit it, Fern. It is too Francine. Look, (counts on her fingers) Frank is pushy and loud and rude and insulting.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Stop helping me, Muffy!

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Fern, cows are females and bulls are males. A cow wouldn't be named Frank, it would be named Fran--(awkward laugh, then he and everyone else but Francine laughs again)

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: That's about as funny as biting an apple and finding half a worm! (drops the paper and leaves in a sulk. Buster looks guilty, Fern looks mean.)

xml.stage: in the teachers' lounge, Mr. Marco is juggling teacups, but they break.

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: (enters, carrying a clipboard) Good morning! Before classes begin, Mr. Haney wants you to know what will you'll be doing for the carnival.

line.speaker: Mr. Ratburn
line.dialogue: (he shows a dragon puppet while Mrs. Tingley looks afraid) A new puppet show.

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: Eek!

line.speaker: Mr. Ratburn
line.dialogue: The Many-Headed Hydra vs Sinbad the Sailor. (as Sinbad:) Whoa!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: I'm going to conduct a happy singalong with a prize for the happiest singer-along-er! (Mrs. Tingley looks concerned and writes on her clipboard, Ms. Sweetwater gets up and speaks in singsong) You don't look very happy! I think I need to sing someone a song right now!

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: (leaving) I think not!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: Oh, come on, let's sing!

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: No!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: (following Mrs. Tingley and playing her guitar, in song) I like fudge, I like fudge, if you ask me what I like, I'd say, "I like fudge".

xml.stage: in the lunch room, Leah MacGrady is stirring a pot of something

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: (whispering) What will you be doing for the carnival?

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: My famous fortune-teller act! (dramatically) You will be taking a trip!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: (singing in the corridor): I like fudge. I like fudge. If you ask me what I like, I say, "I like fudge." (Mrs. Tingley leaves quickly)

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: I didn't mean right now!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: (following Mrs. Tingley) Come on, Miss Tingley! A little song is just what you need! (Mrs. MacGrady looks confused)

xml.stage: Miss Tingley enters Mr. Haney's office and locks the door behind her.

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: Miss Tingley! (Miss Tingley sees Mr. Haney dressed as a magician and looks scared)

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: Eek!

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: You're just in time to rehearse my grand finale when I saw you in half!

line.speaker: Mrs. Tingley
line.dialogue: Me?!

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: Oh, don't worry, it's foolproof! Here, let me demonstrate with this board! (Mrs. Tingley continues to look afraid while Mr. Haney puts the board in his magic box) It only looks like I'm cutting it, but in reality (the board breaks, surprising Mr. Haney, Mrs. Tingley leaves) Oops. Miss Tingley, wait!

xml.stage: in class

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: (hands a piece of paper to Fern) Your comic was so good, I drew one too. Mine's called The Big Clumsy Moose with Big Feet Named Frannie. (it depicts a badly-drawn big-footed moose with a red dress on)

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: Her feet are named Frannie?

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: (walks over) That's funny. I'm gonna do one too!

line.speaker: Binky
line.dialogue: Me too!

line.speaker: Sue Ellen
line.dialogue: I can do a better one than you guys!

line.speaker: The Brain
line.dialogue: Oh, yeah?! (sits back down)

xml.stage: Mrs. Tingley nervously enters the girls' toilets, where Ms. Sweetwater has her guitar

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: Ready to sing?

line.speaker: Ms. Tingley
line.dialogue: Eek!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: (sings:) “Why not smile? Why not smile? Do you think you’re gonna break your face? Why not smile?” (She follows Miss Tingley down the corridor. Behind her comes Mr. Haney with his magic set.)

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: Come back, Miss Tingley!

xml.stage: in the lunchroom, the students look at a picture of Francine as a foghorn

line.speaker: Sue Ellen
line.dialogue: Look, Arthur, Frannie Foghorn!

xml.stage: (Sue Ellen shows Arthur, Muffy, Buster, Fern and the Brain a picture of a frog with Francine's head, Buster and Muffy laugh) Frantic Frog! Fran the stinky sneaker! (she and Muffy giggle) Brain, did you do this one? It's funny! (Arthur looks concernedly at Francine, who's sitting at a table looking peeved)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: I think maybe this is bothering Francine.

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: (mockingly) She'll get over it.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (walks over to Francine's table) Francine, uh...

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (indignantly) Oh, go eat an ant sandwich, Arthur! Cartoons and stones can't break my bones!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: (shows Sue Ellen a piece of paper) Wait, you haven't seen mine!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (running over) Hey, give it to me! I want to read that! (reading) The Giant Slime That Came to School! (we see the comic animated)

line.speaker: Comic Teacher
line.dialogue: (he looks and sounds like Mr. Ratburn and is speaking very quickly) Three billion, six hundred and thirty million two hundred and seventy-nine thousand one hundred and forty-eight divided by seven thousand six hundred and eighty-one! Quickly! Quickly!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) All the kids were at school, learning complicated math! And then it happened!

line.speaker: Giant Slime
line.dialogue: (enters) I'm here!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) A giant slime (the comic students, who look like Arthur, Muffy and Buster, scream) came to school!

line.speaker: Comic Teacher
line.dialogue: Do the math or begone, large slime!

line.speaker: Giant Slime
line.dialogue: Oh yeah?! (eats the teacher)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) The big slime (slurping noise) our teacher! (the teacher screams, but the students cheer) But it wasn't all good. The slime made everyone do its rotten bidding, like play only games it was good at! (the slime pushes around the students and spits a ball through the basketball hoop)

line.speaker: Giant Slime
line.dialogue: P-tooey! I win again!

line.speaker: Comic Students
line.dialogue: This rots!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) Bionic Bunny told it to leave...

line.speaker: Bionic Bunny
line.dialogue: Slime, go away!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: and it (slurping noise) him too! (the slime eats Bionic Bunny, but he and the teacher are alive inside it)

line.speaker: Bionic Bunny
line.dialogue: Know how to play Go Fish?

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) The slime was unstoppable! Until...

line.speaker: Buster-like Cartoon Boy
line.dialogue: Boy, I'm hungry!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: It was dark, so the boy didn't realize he was eating slime! (the boy eats a piece out of the slime)

line.speaker: Cartoon Boy
line.dialogue: Mm, lime! (eats more) Mmm!

line.speaker: Giant Slime
line.dialogue: Cut that out! (leaves, the boy chases it)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: (reading) The boy who would eat anything chased the slime!

line.speaker: Cartoon Boy
line.dialogue: Come back! You're delicious! (eats the slime)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: And he (slurping noise) the whole thing!

line.speaker: Bionic Bunny
line.dialogue: Thank goodness you eat like a pig!

line.speaker: Cartoon Boy
line.dialogue: (shakes Bionic Bunny's hand, he's now much fatter and taller so that the slime can fit in his stomach) You're welcome! (burps and knocks over Bionic Bunny and the teacher)

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: And I would eat a giant slime, if it was lime slime.

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: (enters and plays a horn) The teacher charity carnival is about to begin! (most of the students dash out, dropping the comics on the floor)

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Boy, Francine, you left your comics everywhere!

xml.stage: Francine sighs and leaves, Mrs. MacGrady starts to pick the comics up and looks thoughtful
at the carnival, Miss Bryan, the art teacher makes candy floss that looks like the Brain's face

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Finally, great art you can eat!

xml.stage: (Mr. Ratburn is doing his puppet show. Fern walks by without looking. Mr. Ratburn looks surprised.)
(Mrs. MacGrady is being a fortune teller)

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Fern! I was reading these comics. That Francine sounds just terrible.

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: She's the worst!

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: If you can get all the kids together who drew these, I have a way we can make her look really silly!

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: Sure, I'll be back in a sec.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: It's all part of the fun! When you pull the rope, that pot of green gloop I made will pour on her. Here she comes. Be very quiet and wait 'til I say, "green gloop". (the kids who drew the comics hide behind the curtain) That's your cue to pull the rope and dump the gloop on her.

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: (holding the rope) This is gonna be great. (Fern shh's him, Francine enters)

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Ah, someone who wants their fortune told! Sit down, please and concentrate. (Francine sits) I see a cow and a foghorn.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (sighs) That's me. (her classmates giggle) What's that sound? (they go quiet)

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Nothing. The spirits are restless. And I see a bully bulldozer.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (sighs again) I never saw that one but it's probably me too.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: That's you and a stinky sneaker? That's funny? Yes?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I guess.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: But I see that you are not laughing.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (tearfully) Oh, Mrs. MacGrady! I wish they'd stop making fun of me! I know I shouldn't have called Fern a mouse, but why did they all have to be so mean to me?!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Did you hear that?

line.speaker: Binky
line.dialogue: I can't believe it. Francine has feelings.

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: Yeah!

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Tissues to your left, dear. I see one more thing. Oh, yes, here's a cartoon of you as green gloop. (clears throat) I said there's a cartoon of you as green gloop! (Mrs. MacGrady turns her head to where the students are hiding) Green gloop! (the students pause for a bit; Buster, having his hands on the rope, quietly lets go; the students feel guilty and decides to stop causing their friend further humiliation)

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (blows her nose and sounds even more sad) I just wish they'd stop.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: I think you came to the right fortune teller, dear.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (leaving, choked up) Thank you, Mrs. MacGrady.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Goodbye.

xml.stage: they stand outside the tent

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: It was so fun making fun of her, we didn't think about how it made her feel.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: I always say, "Never serve anyone a stew that you wouldn't want to eat yourself."

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: Wait up, Francine!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Wait up! (he and several others leave)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Oh, by the way, maybe you'd better think about it before you dump that gloop on somebody. It might hurt their feelings.

line.speaker: Mrs. MacGrady
line.dialogue: Maybe you're right. I guess learned something today. Better catch your friends. (Arthur leaves, Mrs. MacGrady goes back into her tent and pulls the rope, and a bunch of balloons, confetti and streamers fall out)

xml.stage: Francine wins at a carnival game

line.speaker: Fern
line.dialogue: Wow, way to go, Francine!

line.speaker: Ms. Sweetwater
line.dialogue: (chasing Mrs. Tingley) Come back, we're about to begin!

line.speaker: Mr. Haney
line.dialogue: (following after) For the hundredth time, Miss Tingley, you have nothing to worry about!
